2.description: Equilibrium price Each panel plots the demand curve, equilibrium price, and supply thresholds, conditional on the underlying state, ωω∈{H,L}⁠, as well as a potential supply curve.

3.description: Posterior beliefs This diagram shows the beliefs of the marginal investor, q(Fi)⁠, given pE=E(Fi)⁠, and the manager, q(Fm)⁠, for each price and realization of the manager’s private signal.

4.description: Numerical illustration: Risk shifting and debt overhang The figure plots the project NPV, NPVE⁠, K, K0, λ and the probability of inefficient investment (⁠σP1(σ)⁠) under different learning environments as a function of σ. Other key parameter values are set to ρρρ=0.7, ρm=0.55⁠, γ = 0, and c = 0.06; the distributions GH and GL are exponential with parameters λλH=1 and λλL=2⁠, respectively; and F = 0.5. The blue region corresponds to risk shifting (i.e., σσ>0⁠), and the white region corresponds to debt overhang (i.e., σσ<0⁠).

B.1.description: Plot of optimal λE and λD as a function of σ This plot shows the measure of informed equity investors (λ) and the precision of manager’s information (ρm) as a function of σ. The other parameters are γ = 0, F = 0.6, ρρ=0.85⁠, and c = 0.05, and the distributions GH and GL are exponential with parameters 1 and 2, respectively.

B.2.description: Plot of NPVE(.) as a function of σ The figure shows the value of the investment to the manager as a function of project characteristic σ. The other parameters are γ = 0, ρρρ=0.7, ρm=0.55⁠, c = 0.06, and EH,NI−EL,NI=1⁠.

B.3.description: Plot of optimal λE and λD as a function of σ The figure shows the mass of informed equity and debt investors as a function of project characteristic σ. The other parameters are γ = 0, F = 0.6, ρρ=0.8⁠, and c = 0.05, the distributions GH and GL are exponential with parameters 1 and 2, respectively.
